---
title: "Coursework"
toc: true
---

> Exact assignment prompts, rubrics, due dates, submission links, and
> conference sign-ups live in **Blackboard**. This page is a
> high-level outline of how work is organized across the term.

## What the course produces

Across the semester, the course produces work in four overlapping
forms.

### Weekly Blackboard assignments

Most weeks include a short Blackboard assignment — usually a Quarto
document, a LaTeX writeup, or a small technical report — designed to
take 30–90 minutes outside of any optional support time. Each one is
graded inside the four course categories described on the
[syllabus](syllabus.qmd).

The **best 9 of 11** weekly labs/homework count for ordinary
weekly-work credit. **HW 11 and HW 12 are the dedicated AI module
assignments in the Generative AI Literacy category and are not
droppable.** Projects, required conferences, AI verification
components, and the final portfolio are also not droppable.

### Two projects

- A **LaTeX Project** in the LaTeX block (Weeks 2–6). The Week 4
  LaTeX checkpoint conference confirms scope and direction.
- An **R Project** in the R block (Weeks 7–10). The Week 7 R
  transition conference and the Week 10 R Project conference frame
  the project.

### Five required conferences

Short one-on-one conferences in Weeks 1, 4, 7, 10, and 13. Each is
10–15 minutes — a practical progress check, not an oral exam. See the
[syllabus](syllabus.qmd#required-conferences) for the full conference
list and what each one is for.

### Final portfolio + reflection

A small, organized collection of the term's artifacts plus a 1–2
page written reflection, submitted in Week 15 through Blackboard.

## Where each thing lives

- **This site (public course site).** Lessons, conceptual notes,
  software setup guidance, walkthroughs, examples, and shared
  resources. Read it the way you would read a course text.
- **Blackboard (operational course home).** Assignment prompts,
  rubrics, due dates, submission inboxes, conference sign-ups, the
  gradebook, and official announcements.

If anything on the public site conflicts with Blackboard, the
Blackboard version wins.

## Late work and portfolio organization

Late-work expectations and portfolio-organization expectations are
summarized in the [syllabus](syllabus.qmd#late-work). Exact dates and
submission links are in Blackboard.
